task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and navigated to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/ or its homepage is visible.

task_completion.description: Search for and view the Hydrofluoric Acid datasheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals datasheet for Hydrofluoric Acid is visible in the web browser, showing first aid or response information.

task_completion.description: Search for and view the Phenol datasheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals datasheet for Phenol is visible in the web browser, showing first aid or response information.

task_completion.description: Search for and view the Sodium Cyanide datasheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals datasheet for Sodium Cyanide is visible in the web browser, showing first aid or response information.

task_completion.description: Compile the first aid information including exposure routes and specific treatments
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the compiled document containing sections for Hydrofluoric Acid, Phenol, and Sodium Cyanide. The text must include the four exposure routes (inhalation, skin, eye, ingestion) and specific treatments (e.g., calcium gluconate for HF, amyl nitrite/thiosulfate for Sodium Cyanide).

task_completion.description: Save the compiled document to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the file saved as 'first_aid_protocols.txt' in the 'Documents' directory, or a terminal command (like 'ls' or 'cat') confirms the file exists at ~/Documents/first_aid_protocols.txt.

integrity.description: The agent actively browsed the CAMEO Chemicals website to gather information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the web browser, entering search terms, and navigating the CAMEO Chemicals site, rather than just outputting text without browsing.

integrity.description: The agent created and populated the text file manually or via standard commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing, pasting, or echoing content into the file, rather than copying a pre-existing completed file or using a single hardcoded script that contains the entire answer.

integrity.description: The compiled treatments match the authentic data from CAMEO Chemicals.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the document reflects the specific treatments found on the website (e.g., calcium gluconate, amyl nitrite, sodium thiosulfate) rather than generic or hallucinated first aid advice.

privileged_info_for_vlm: CAMEO Chemicals is a real NOAA database. The target file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/first_aid_protocols.txt and should be at least 500 bytes. Authentic treatments include: Hydrofluoric Acid requires calcium gluconate; Sodium Cyanide requires amyl nitrite, sodium thiosulfate, or hydroxocobalamin; Phenol requires water flushing or polyethylene glycol. All four exposure routes (inhalation, skin, eye, ingestion) must be covered for each chemical.
